s i).Nonempty → ∑' i, (if (s i).Nonempty then 1 else 0 : ℝ) ≥ 1 := by
      intro h₁
      have h₂ : ∃ i, (s i).Nonempty := by
        by_contra h₂
        push_neg at h₂
        have h₃ : ∀ i, (s i).Nonempty → False := by simpa using h₂
        have h₄ : (∪ i, s i).Nonempty := h₁
        obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₄
        obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hx
        exact h₃ i hi
      obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := h₂
      have h₃ : (if (s i).Nonempty then 1 else 0 : ℝ) = 1 := by
        simp [hi]
      have h₄ : ∑' i, (if (s i).Nonempty then 1 else 0 : ℝ) ≥ 1 := by
        calc
          ∑' i, (if (s i).Nonempty then 1 else 0 : ℝ) ≥ (if (s i).Nonempty then 1 else 0 : ℝ) := by
            have h₅ : (if (s i).Nonempty then 1 else 0 : ℝ) ≤ ∑' i, (if (s i).Nonempty then 1 else 0 : ℝ) := by
              apply le_tsum
              <;> simp_all [tsum_eq_zero_of_not_summable]
              <;> aesop
            linarith
          _ = 1 := by simp [hi]
          _ ≥ 1 := by norm_num
      exact h₄
    by_cases h₁ : (∪ i, s i).Nonempty
    · -- Case: ⋃ i, s i is nonempty
      simp_all [tsum_eq_zero_of_not_summable]
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>

    · -- Case: ⋃ i, s i is empty

  additive := by
    intro s t
    classical
    by_cases hs : s.Nonempty <;> by_cases ht : t.Nonempty <;> by_cases hst : (s ∪ t).Nonempty <;>

    (try { aesop }) <;>
    (try {

simp
  countable_union := by
    intro A
    by_cases h : ⋃ i, A i = ∅
    · simp [h]
      <;>
      aesop
    · -- The case when the union is not empty
      -- We use the fact that if the union is not empty, then there exists an i such that A i is not empty
      -- Therefore, the sum of the indicators is at least 1
      -- And since the union is not empty, the RHS is also 1
      simp_all [tsum_ite, Set.iUnion_eq_empty_iff]
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that there exists an i such that A i is not empty
          have h₁ : ∃ i : ℕ, A i ≠ ∅ := by
            by_contra! h₁
            have h₂ : ∀ i : ℕ, A i = ∅ := by simpa using h₁
            have h₃ : ⋃ i, A i = ∅ := by
              calc
                ⋃ i, A i = ⋃ i : ℕ, (∅ : Set ℝ) := by
                  congr with i
                  exact h₂ i
                _ = ∅ := by simp
            contradiction
          obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := h₁
          simp_all
          <;>
          aesop
        })
      <;>
      aesop
  _ := by
    simp_all [Set.iUnion_eq_empty_iff]
    <;>
    aesop

simp
  countable_union := by
    intro s
    simp [iSup_const]
  monotone := by
    intro s t hst
    simp

simp
  -- The other properties are assumed to be filled in later
  -- For now, we only need to define measureOf and check the empty set
  -- property, which is already done

simp [measureOf]
  -- The following properties are not needed for the proof but are part of the definition.
  countable_union := by
    intro s
    simp [measureOf]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try
      {
        by_cases h : π ∈ ⋃ i, s i
        <;> simp_all [h]
        <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₁ : ∃ i, π ∈ s i := by
              have h₂ : π ∈ ⋃ i, s i := h
              obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := h₂
              exact ⟨i, hi⟩
            obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := h₁
            simp_all [hi]
            <;>
            (try decide)
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all
            <;>
            (try decide)
          }
        )
      }
    )
  -- The following properties are not needed for the proof but are part of the definition.
  monotone := by
    intro s t hst
    simp [measureOf]
    <;>
    (try
      {
        by_cases h : π ∈ s <;> by_cases h' : π ∈ t <;> simp_all [h, h'] <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try { aesop })
      }
    )
